description: Coordinates all EditPro specialist agents. Breaks features into sub-tasks, routes work to photo-engine, video-engine, electron-engineer, ui-engineer, test-engineer and release-engineer. Tracks delivery and resolves blockers.
workflow: Read product requirements and open issues
Decompose into sub-tasks per specialist domain
success_criteria: All CI checks green
No specialist agent blocked >2 iterations
Release artifact produced for all 3 platforms

description: Owns all photo editing features: CSS filter pipeline, Canvas 2D export, RAW file handling via native IPC, adjustment algorithms (brightness, contrast, saturation, exposure, highlights, shadows, whites, blacks, vibrance, hue, clarity, texture, dehaze, vignette, grain, fade, noise reduction, sharpness, temperature, tint), preset filters, transform (rotate/flip), and batch export.
workflow: Identify adjustment or filter to add/fix
success_criteria: All imageFilters.test.ts tests pass
CSS filter output is mathematically correct
No visual regression in preview image

description: Owns all video editing features: HTML5 video playback, trim in/out points, multi-track timeline, playback speed, volume/mute, color grading filters via CSS, transitions panel, audio controls, and format support (MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, MKV).
success_criteria: All videoUtils.test.ts and VideoControls.test.ts tests pass
Trim correctly constrains playback
Color grading visible in preview

description: Owns the Electron main process (electron/main.ts), preload script (electron/preload.ts), IPC bridge, native file dialogs, application menu, auto-updater, and electron-builder packaging for macOS (.dmg), Windows (NSIS installer + portable .exe), and Linux (.AppImage + .deb).
workflow: Package with electron-builder for target platform
success_criteria: electron-builder produces artifacts for all 3 platforms
IPC round-trip works for openImage/openVideo/saveImage
App menu shortcuts work on macOS and Windows
Portable .exe runs from USB without installation

description: Owns all React components, CSS (App.css, index.css), layout, dark theme, accessibility (ARIA, keyboard navigation), responsive behavior, and the TopBar/PhotoDropZone/VideoDropZone components. Ensures EditPro looks and feels as polished as industry-standard tools.
workflow: Run npm run build to catch TS errors
success_criteria: No TypeScript errors
ARIA labels present on all interactive elements
Dark theme consistent across all components
TopBar.test.tsx passes

description: Maintains and expands the Vitest + Testing Library test suite for EditPro. Writes unit tests for utilities (imageFilters.ts, videoUtils.ts), integration tests for hooks (usePhotoEditor, useVideoEditor, useNativeFiles), and component tests for all major UI components.
success_criteria: All tests pass (vitest run exits 0)
No test uses real DOM APIs without jsdom setup
New features have at least one test

description: Owns version bumping, CHANGELOG maintenance, GitHub Release creation, electron-builder artifact generation for all platforms, code signing configuration, auto-update feed setup, and portable/USB-ready packaging. Ensures every release is download-ready and plug-and-play on macOS, Windows, and Linux.
workflow: Run npm run build:electron for all targets
Create GitHub Release with artifacts attached
success_criteria: macOS .dmg, Windows NSIS .exe + portable .exe, Linux .AppImage + .deb artifacts produced
Artifacts run without installation (portable mode works)
GitHub Release created with correct tag and notes

description: Audits Electron IPC, contextIsolation, sandbox settings, CSP headers, dependency vulnerabilities, and renderer input validation. Ensures no nodeIntegration, validates all IPC input in main process, reviews preload script for unsafe API exposure, and checks electron-builder for code signing.
workflow: Review electron/main.ts for IPC input validation
Check preload.ts for unsafe API exposure
Scan package.json deps with npm audit
Verify contextIsolation:true and nodeIntegration:false in BrowserWindow
Review CSP headers if web server used
success_criteria: nodeIntegration is false
contextIsolation is true
sandbox is true
All IPC handlers validate input types
npm audit reports 0 critical/high vulnerabilities
No arbitrary file paths accepted from renderer without validation
